response_text:
**Internal Memo**

**To:** All Employees  
**From:** Leadership Team  
**Date:** [Insert Date]  
**Subject:** New Remote Work Policy

As our company continues to grow, we are updating our remote work policy to better support collaboration, flexibility, and consistency across the organization. Effective **[insert effective date]**, we will move to a **hybrid remote work model** for all employees whose roles can be performed off-site.

## What the new policy is

Under the new policy:

- Employees will be expected to work **in the office at least two days per week**.
- The remaining workdays may be completed remotely, subject to team needs and manager approval.
- Employees and managers should agree on regular in-office days to ensure overlap for meetings, project work, and team collaboration.
- Some roles may require a different schedule based on business or customer needs. Managers will communicate those exceptions directly.
- Core working hours will remain **9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. local time**, unless otherwise approved, to make collaboration easier across teams.
- All employees, whether working remotely or in the office, are expected to remain available on Slack, attend scheduled meetings, and follow standard security and data protection practices.

## Why we are making this change

Over the past several years, remote work has given our teams greater flexibility and helped many employees maintain a strong work-life balance. At the same time, we have seen that certain kinds of work benefit from more regular in-person interaction, especially:

- onboarding and mentoring new employees,
- cross-functional planning,
- design and product collaboration,
- team building and company culture, and
- faster problem-solving on complex projects.

As a 120-person software company, we are at a stage where we need both flexibility and more predictable ways of working together. This policy is intended to create a clearer structure while still preserving the benefits of remote work.

## What employees need to do next

Please
